solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int findIntegers(int n) {
        int[] dp = new int[32];
        dp[0] = 1;
        dp[1] = 2;
        for (int i = 2; i < 31; i++) {
            dp[i] = dp[i - 1] + dp[i - 2];
        }

        int result = 0;
        int prevBit = 0;
        for (int i = 29; i >= 0; i--) {
            int mask = 1 << i;
            int bit = (n & mask) >> i;
            if (bit == 1) {
                result += dp[i];
                if (prevBit == 1) {
                    return result;
                }
                prevBit = 1;
            } else {
                prevBit = 0;
            }
        }
        return result + 1;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses dynamic programming to precompute the number of valid binary strings of length i that do not contain consecutive ones. It then iterates through the bits of n, using the precomputed values to count valid numbers less than or equal to n. The algorithm avoids checking each number individually, making it efficient for large n.
